name: Unauthorized Remote Access Tool Detection
description: Detects and blocks unauthorized remote access tools (AnyDesk, TeamViewer, UltraViewer, RustDesk, etc.) running on managed endpoints. When a remote access tool is detected, the playbook checks it against the approved tools list and analyzes the execution context. Unauthorized tools are immediately killed, blocked via EDR policy, and a compliance ticket is created. If the tool was actively tunneling to an external IP, Riggs AI performs lateral movement analysis to assess potential compromise.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Kill unauthorized processes, block application hashes, and isolate endpoint if needed

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create policy violation tickets for unauthorized remote access tool usage

config.description: Triggers when a remote access tool is detected running on a managed endpoint

config.code:
process_name = alert.get('process_name', '').lower()
app_name = alert.get('application_name', '').lower()
approved_remote_tools = [t.lower() for t in context.get('approved_remote_tools', [])]
is_approved = any(t in process_name or t in app_name for t in approved_remote_tools)
has_active_tunnel = alert.get('has_active_connection', False)
remote_ip = alert.get('remote_ip', '')
result = {'process_name': process_name, 'app_name': alert.get('application_name'), 'is_approved': is_approved, 'has_active_tunnel': has_active_tunnel, 'remote_ip': remote_ip, 'file_hash': alert.get('file_hash', '')}
config.description: Check the remote access tool against the list of approved tools and detect active tunnels
Routes based on whether the remote access tool is on the approved list

parameters.note: Remote access tool {{code_1.app_name}} on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} is approved. No action required.

config.description: Log the approved remote access tool usage and close the alert
Terminate the unauthorized remote access tool and add its hash to the block list
Routes based on whether the tool had an active remote connection

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this unauthorized remote access tool for signs of active compromise. The tool had an active tunnel to an external IP. Determine if this is an attacker using the tool for C2 or data exfiltration, or a user circumventing policy.
config.description: AI-powered lateral movement analysis to assess if the active tunnel indicates compromise
Isolate the endpoint due to active remote tunnel and notify SOC for investigation

parameters.title: Unauthorized Remote Access Tool: {{code_1.app_name}} — {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}}

config.description: Create a policy violation ticket for the unauthorized remote access tool
Notify the SOC about the unauthorized remote access tool and actions taken
Remote access tool detection workflow complete
